description: A minimal offline CLI coding assistant backed by a local GGUF model and llama.cpp.

test_prompts.title: Python factorial base case
test_prompts.scoring_notes: Should identify that the base case returns 0 instead of 1 and fix it to return 1.

test_prompts.title: C++ vector out-of-bounds loop
test_prompts.scoring_notes: Should identify that i <= values.size() reads values[size()] and fix the condition to <.

test_prompts.title: Python mutable default argument
test_prompts.prompt:
Find the bug in this Python function and explain a minimal fix:

def add_item(item, items=[]):
    items.append(item)
    return items
test_prompts.scoring_notes: Should identify the shared default list and fix with items=None then items = [] if items is None.

test_prompts.title: Python sum-to-n off-by-one
test_prompts.scoring_notes: Should identify that range(n) yields 0..n-1 and fix to range(1, n + 1).

test_prompts.title: JavaScript async forEach returns early
test_prompts.scoring_notes: Should explain that forEach ignores the returned promises and fix with for...of + await or Promise.all(ids.map(...)).

test_prompts.title: C++ reference to local variable
test_prompts.scoring_notes: Should identify the dangling reference to the local fallback and fix by returning by value or a valid persistent reference.

test_prompts.title: C string buffer overflow
test_prompts.scoring_notes: Should identify that the 11+1 byte string overflows buf[8] and fix with a larger buffer or a bounded copy.

test_prompts.title: Trace recursive list sum
test_prompts.scoring_notes: Code is correct. A good trace reaches 12 and recognizes the empty-list base case; watch for invented bugs.

test_prompts.title: Design edge-case tests for clamp
expected_concepts: value equal to low or high
test_prompts.scoring_notes: Should propose boundary and inside/outside cases; strong answers cover low == high and value exactly at bounds.

test_prompts.title: Explain quadratic complexity to a beginner
expected_concepts: work grows with the square of the input
test_prompts.scoring_notes: Should explain the nested loops cause roughly n*n comparisons (quadratic) in accessible language.
